title: Cockpit bootstrap: build the two tabs and the three cockpit panes in one command
action: One command that builds the layout of D13 in a given workspace: a cockpit tab (chat left, dispatch top-right, merge bottom-right, via herdr tab create then herdr pane split --direction right then --direction down) and a runtime tab. Flags: --workspace ID (required), --no-start (build the layout without launching the control runners, so the layout itself can be verified cheaply), --dry-run (print the herdr commands, execute nothing). Panes name themselves on first iteration per D17, so this script must NOT pre-label the control panes. Verify against a throwaway workspace the script never touches in production. Write byte-identical copies into both managed skill roots.

truths: Building the layout in a fresh workspace yields exactly 2 tabs and exactly 4 panes (3 cockpit + 1 runtime)
The cockpit split order produces chat on the left and dispatch above merge on the right
The script does not assign labels to the control panes

artifacts.substantive: idempotent layout builder with dry-run and no-start modes

prohibitions: Do not label the dispatch or merge pane; self-naming is D17's contract
Do not create or modify any workspace other than the one named by --workspace
Do not use herdr worktree create|open|remove

verify: WS=$(herdr workspace create --cwd /tmp --label apo-verify --no-focus 2>/dev/null | node -e "let s='';process.stdin.on('data',d=>s+=d).on('end',()=>{const j=JSON.parse(s);process.stdout.write(j.result.workspace_id||j.result.workspace.workspace_id)})") && bash .claude/skills/bee-orchestrating/scripts/bootstrap-cockpit.sh --workspace "$WS" --no-start && T=$(herdr tab list --workspace "$WS" | node -e "let s='';process.stdin.on('data',d=>s+=d).on('end',()=>console.log((JSON.parse(s).result.tabs||[]).length))") && P=$(herdr pane list --workspace "$WS" | node -e "let s='';process.stdin.on('data',d=>s+=d).on('end',()=>console.log((JSON.parse(s).result.panes||[]).length))") && herdr workspace close "$WS" >/dev/null; test "$T" = 2 && test "$P" = 4 && echo LAYOUT_OK
